regime: Risk-On Trend Day 2 / Equities Only (NQ and ES Break to New Records)
summary: Day two of the trend, and it is equities only. MNQ cleared the 30,000 psych to a 30,077 high and closed +0.46% above a rising 29,814 stop; MES broke the 7,602 cash record to a 7,628 high, +0.52%, into fresh price discovery. The move was quiet and orderly, ATR still contracting, the signature of a trend rather than an expansion. Gold and crude did not come along: on the correct MGC1! front contract gold sits below its 4,142 stop, and crude held its flipped short to a 70.77 low. The book can hold REDUCED and lean with the equity trend, but it is Friday into a weekend and CPI (June) lands Tuesday, so size for the gap risk, not the breakout.

keyLevels.label: MNQ ATR Stop
keyLevels.note: Rising LONG; RTH close +255 above. The line that defines the day-2 trend.

keyLevels.label: MNQ AVWAP / RTH High
keyLevels.note: Closed above AVWAP; cleared the 30,000 psych, now support.

keyLevels.label: MES ATR Stop / Record
keyLevels.note: Long; broke the 7,602 cash record to a 7,628 high, into price discovery.

keyLevels.label: MGC ATR Stop (MGC1!)
keyLevels.note: FLIPPED SHORT on the front contract; close 4,128.6, -13 below. Diverged from equities.

keyLevels.label: MCL ATR Stop
keyLevels.note: FLIPPED SHORT; close 71.51, -1.49 below. Pressed to a 70.77 low.

setups.name: TSMOM-20 1hr (long)
setups.entry: Long with the trend on a pullback to the rising AVWAP (29,988), not a chase at the 30,077 high
setups.bias: IN_HABITAT long. Day two of a clean equity trend, both indices at new highs; still size for the weekend and CPI Tuesday.

setups.name: AVWAP Bounce (MNQ / MES)
setups.entry: Long on a hold of the rising session AVWAP (MNQ 29,988, MES 7,609) as support
setups.bias: IN_HABITAT. AVWAP is rising support on a trend day; a loss is the first stall signal.

setups.name: E3v2 Momentum Long (15min)
setups.entry: Long on a 15min breakout with the regime filter green
setups.bias: IN_HABITAT. Risk-on trend qualifies the filter; a second clean day of breakout tape.

event.name: No scheduled Tier-1 release (quiet pre-weekend Friday)
event.actual: No catalyst; price, not news, ran the tape into the weekend.
event.nqReaction: MNQ broke its 29,937 OR high on the 13:45 UTC drive and never gave it back, clearing 30,000 to a 30,077 high on a quiet, orderly day-2 trend.
event.crossAsset: MES was the tell: a false OR down-break at 14:30 reversed and reclaimed, then broke the 7,602 cash record into price discovery. Gold and crude diverged lower, gold closing below its 4,142 stop and crude pressing to a 70.77 low.

observe.name: GAP_NQ (gap fade)
observe.note: New-high close into a weekend; a Monday gap up runs into open air above 30,000, a gap down fills toward the 29,814 stop. No clean fade yet.

observe.name: TSMOM252LO_NQ (long-only 252)
observe.note: Long-horizon base and short-horizon trend aligned long; the trend has extended two sessions.

observe.name: ES confirmation
observe.note: MES and MNQ both made new highs on rising stops; the record break is real participation, not a stop artifact.

risk.throttleState: REDUCED, leaning toward NORMAL on equities. Day two of a clean trend with both indices at new highs and volatility contracting supports modest size, but it is Friday into a weekend with CPI Tuesday, so do not carry oversized risk over the gap.
risk.ddProximity: Not binding; the book entered flat and the full daily budget is intact.
risk.sizing: A TSMOM-20 long stands with the trend above a rising 29,814 stop; a starter-to-half on a pullback to the 29,988 AVWAP is reasonable rather than a chase at 30,077. Gold and crude shorts are with their flipped stops but counter to the equity tape; keep them small and independent.
risk.note: Week ahead: CPI (June) lands Tuesday July 14 at 8:30 ET, the gate on this trend; a weekend of headline risk sits between Friday's close and Monday's open. Monday is a normal session (BIR sits out on Mondays). Let pullbacks to the rising AVWAP set entries and size for the gap, not the breakout.

psychology: Two traps on a record-break day. The first is chasing the breakout at new highs because you are afraid to miss the discovery move; the second is the divergence trap, forcing a long in gold because equities are ripping. Gold is below its stop and did not participate, so trade it on its own line, not on the S&P's. The equity trend is defined by the rising stops (MNQ 29,814, MES 7,589); let a pullback to the rising AVWAP set the entry and let price come to you. It is Friday into a weekend with CPI Tuesday, so the discipline that matters most today is sizing, not direction.

fullReport.title: Momentum Breakout (Regime Filter)
fullReport.html:
FieldRead
Regime filterPositive and extending: price above AVWAP, above rising long stops, ATR contracting. A second clean trend day.
Trend edgeTSMOM-20 long confirmed on both indices via new highs, not a stop ratchet; the Jul 8 flip has run two sessions.
ContinuationQualifies long on pullbacks to the rising AVWAP (MNQ 29,988), not a chase into new highs.
E3v2 Momentum LongStays green in the risk-on trend; the 15min breakout filter is qualifying.

fullReport.html:

Sigma73
+1s / +2s / +3s30,056 / 30,130 / 30,203
-1s / -2s / -3s29,909 / 29,836 / 29,763
SignalPrice closed 30,069 just above the +1s, riding the band up on a trend day; mean-reversion fades are counter-trend and OUT of habitat today. A fade needs a stretched +3s (30,203) tag with an exhaustion bar, half size at most. Price-based (volume unavailable on the continuous contract).

yesterdaysMapScore.note: Graded the Jul 9 map (targeting Jul 10). Rows 1, 3 and 5 TRIGGERED / PLAYED OUT (MNQ cleared 30,000 to 30,077; MES broke the 7,602 record to 7,628; MCL extended to a 70.77 low). Row 4 TRIGGERED / FAILED (gold's long-continuation failed; on the corrected MGC1! front contract it closed below its 4,142 stop and flipped short, and the Jul 9 gold levels were MGC2!-contaminated). Row 2 NOT TRIGGERED (MNQ never neared the 29,637 stop).
